title: zehneria guamensis
intro: Zehneria guamensis, also known as Guam creeper or kava, is a plant species native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in Guam and other Micronesian islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antispasmodic, anxiolytic, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Zehneria guamensis include reducing anxiety and stress, relieving muscle and joint pain, and promoting relaxation. Traditionally, the plant's roots are harvested and used to prepare a tea or infusion, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
